name: Chicken Fried Steak
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds Top Round or Chuck Steak
2 cups Saltine cracker crumbs, rolled fine
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together 3/4 cup of flour, salt, and pepper.
2. Pound the mixture onto both sides of the meat with the edge of a heavy plate or mallet until evenly coated.
3. Cut the meat into serving-size pieces.
4. Beat the eggs with cream in a shallow dish.
5. Heat vegetable oil in a heavy iron skillet over moderately high heat.
6. Reserve 3 tablespoons of flour for later use.
7. Dredge each steak in the reserved flour, dip in egg mixture, and then coat with cracker crumbs.
8. Add steaks to the hot oil in batches. Brown the steaks on both sides until well done but not dry, about 2-3 minutes per side.
9. Reduce heat to medium, cover skillet, and cook for 15-to-20 minutes, turning occasionally, until cooked through and tender.
10. Remove steaks from pan and drain on paper towels.
11. To make gravy: Pour off all but 3 tablespoons of fat in the skillet. Stir in 3 tablespoons flour, cooking until smooth.
12. Add cream and chicken stock, stirring to incorporate any particles. Adjust seasoning if needed.
13. Slice meat across grain and top with gravy. Serve hot with mashed potatoes, homemade biscuits, or cornbread, and a green vegetable.
